quotes.text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: To see what is right and not do it is the want of courage.
quotes.author: Confucius

quotes.text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
quotes.author: Laozi

quotes.text: All warfare is based on deception.
quotes.author: Sun Tzu

quotes.text: The only constant in life is change.
quotes.author: Heraclitus

quotes.text: What is, is.
quotes.author: Parmenides

quotes.text: You have power over your mind — not outside events.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
quotes.author: Epictetus

quotes.text: It is better to be feared than loved, if you cannot be both.
quotes.author: Niccolò Machiavelli

quotes.text: The life of man [is] solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short.
quotes.author: Thomas Hobbes

quotes.text: Where there is no law, there is no freedom.
quotes.author: John Locke

quotes.text: Act only according to that maxim whereby you can, at the same time, will that it should become a universal law.
quotes.author: Immanuel Kant

quotes.text: Reason is, and ought only to be the slave of the passions.
quotes.author: David Hume

quotes.text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
quotes.author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quotes.text: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
quotes.author: Søren Kierkegaard

quotes.text: Philosophers have only interpreted the world; the point, however, is to change it.
quotes.author: Karl Marx

quotes.text: The sad truth is that most evil is done by people who never make up their minds to be good or evil.
quotes.author: Hannah Arendt

quotes.text: Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power.
quotes.author: Abraham Lincoln

quotes.text: The most incomprehensible thing about the universe is that it is comprehensible.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: What we observe is not nature itself, but nature exposed to our method of questioning.
quotes.author: Werner Heisenberg

quotes.text: Either mathematics is too big for the human mind, or the human mind is more than a machine.
quotes.author: Kurt Gödel

quotes.text: We can only see a short distance ahead, but we can see plenty there that needs to be done.
quotes.author: Alan Turing

quotes.text: Information is the resolution of uncertainty.
quotes.author: Claude Shannon

quotes.text: I would rather have questions that can't be answered than answers that can't be questioned.
quotes.author: Richard Feynman

quotes.text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
quotes.author: Carl Sagan

quotes.text: The heart has its reasons which reason knows nothing of.
quotes.author: Blaise Pascal

quotes.text: Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
quotes.author: Simone Weil

quotes.text: The real test of a man is not how he behaves in moments of comfort, but how he stands at times of controversy and challenge.
quotes.author: Vaclav Havel

quotes.text: Resentment is like drinking poison and then hoping it will kill your enemies.
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.
quotes.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

quotes.text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
quotes.author: Marie Curie

quotes.text: DITEMPA, BUKAN DIBERI.
quotes.author: Arif Fazil

quotes.text: In any consistent formal system, there are statements that are true but unprovable.
quotes.author: Kurt Gödel

quotes.text: The concept of truth cannot be defined within the language to which it applies.
quotes.author: Alfred Tarski

quotes.text: The whole problem with the world is that fools and fanatics are always so certain of themselves.
quotes.author: Bertrand Russell

quotes.text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
quotes.author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quotes.text: The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
quotes.author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quotes.text: Science must begin with myths, and with the criticism of myths.
quotes.author: Karl Popper

quotes.text: Normal science does not aim at novelties of fact or theory and, when successful, finds none.
quotes.author: Thomas Kuhn

quotes.text: Research programmes may be progressive or degenerating.
quotes.author: Imre Lakatos

quotes.text: The only principle that does not inhibit progress is: anything goes.
quotes.author: Paul Feyerabend

quotes.text: Instead of trying to produce a programme to simulate the adult mind, why not rather try to produce one which simulates the child's?
quotes.author: Alan Turing

quotes.text: Anyone who considers arithmetical methods of producing random digits is, of course, in a state of sin.
quotes.author: John von Neumann

quotes.text: You cannot prove that a program is elegant.
quotes.author: Gregory Chaitin

quotes.text: The fundamental problem of communication is that of reproducing at one point either exactly or approximately a message selected at another point.
quotes.author: Claude Shannon

quotes.text: We are but whirlpools in a river of ever-flowing water.
quotes.author: Norbert Wiener

quotes.text: Order out of chaos.
quotes.author: Ilya Prigogine

quotes.text: The absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: I think, therefore I am.
quotes.author: René Descartes

quotes.text: Freedom is understanding necessity.
quotes.author: Baruch Spinoza

quotes.text: Man is equally incapable of seeing the nothingness from which he emerges and the infinity in which he is engulfed.
quotes.author: Blaise Pascal

quotes.text: Man can do what he wills but he cannot will what he wills.
quotes.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

quotes.text: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design.
quotes.author: Friedrich Hayek

quotes.text: There is only one way to avoid criticism: do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.
quotes.author: Laozi

quotes.text: The man who asks a question is a fool for a minute, the man who does not ask is a fool for life.
quotes.author: Confucius

quotes.text: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
quotes.author: Sun Tzu

quotes.text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: Fortune favors the bold.
quotes.author: Virgil

quotes.text: A room without books is like a body without a soul.
quotes.author: Cicero

quotes.text: Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.
quotes.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

quotes.text: Hell is other people.
quotes.author: Jean-Paul Sartre

quotes.text: One death is a tragedy; one million is a statistic.
quotes.author: Joseph Stalin

quotes.text: Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.
quotes.author: George Santayana

quotes.text: The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.
quotes.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

quotes.text: It is no measure of health to be well adjusted to a profoundly sick society.
quotes.author: J. Krishnamurti

quotes.text: We do not see things as they are, we see them as we are.
quotes.author: Anaïs Nin

quotes.text: Courage is not the absence of fear, but rather the assessment that something else is more important than fear.
quotes.author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

quotes.text: Prediction is very difficult, especially if it's about the future.
quotes.author: Niels Bohr
